content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 12, 2008

William Cavala
1429 Perkins Way
Sacramento, CA 95818

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-08-124

Dear Mr. Cavala:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Mariko Yamada
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your
questions involve the reporting of a payment made by Ms. Yamada to settle a legal
dispute with a former campaign worker.

The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose
duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative.
(Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a) (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission
may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires
us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)
Additionally, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if providing advice
would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest. (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(F).)

After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written
advice to you at this time. The questions you have posed are related to a past
enforcement action, FPPC Case No. 08/351, resolved by the Commission's Enforcement
Division with an Advisory Letter to Ms. Yamada dated July 1, 2008. Accordingly,
issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and would not further the
impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

As instructed in the Advisory Letter to Ms. Yamada, the committee's campaign
statement should be amended immediately to reflect the payment to settle the dispute as a

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. W-08-124
Page No. 2

campaign expenditure. Furthermore, because Ms. Yamada has previously made a
payment of personal funds to the settle the dispute, the committee's campaign statement
should be amended immediately to reflect a contribution from Ms. Yamada to the
committee equal to the amount of the payment made to settle the dispute.

You may contact our Technical Assistance Division if you need additional
assistance with the necessary amendments. If you believe you can provide additional
facts relating to the past enforcement action, FPPC Case No. 08/351, you may provide
these facts to our Enforcement Division for further consideration.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl
